prompts: A transformer operates on the principle of electromagnetic induction, where an alternating current (AC) passing through the primary coil generates a changing magnetic field. This changing magnetic field induces an electromotive force (EMF) in the secondary coil, producing an AC voltage. The voltage level can be increased or decreased depending on the ratio of the number of turns in the primary coil to the number of turns in the secondary coil. This allows transformers to efficiently transfer electrical energy between circuits at different voltage levels, playing a crucial role in electrical power distribution.
